BasicsPage.title: Election Basics
BasicsPage.subtitle: Understand how the Indian election process works step-by-step in simple language.

step1.title: What is an election?
step1.description: An election is a democratic process where citizens choose their representatives who will run the government and make decisions on their behalf.

step2.title: Who can vote?
step2.description: Any Indian citizen who is 18 years of age or older, and whose name is in the voter list, has the right to vote.

step3.title: What is ECI?
step3.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an independent body that ensures elections are free and fair across the country.

step4.title: What is an EVM?
step4.description: An Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) is a device used to record votes electronically instead of using paper ballots.

step5.title: What is VVPAT?
step5.description: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) is a machine attached to the EVM that prints a paper slip so you can verify your vote was cast correctly.

step6.title: What is NOTA?
step6.description: NOTA stands for 'None of the Above'. It is an option on the EVM for voters who do not wish to vote for any of the candidates.

HowToVotePage.title: How to Vote
HowToVotePage.subtitle: A step-by-step visual guide on what to expect at the polling booth.

step1.title: Go to Polling Booth
step1.description: Find your assigned polling booth online or via the Voter Helpline app and go there on election day.

step2.title: Show your ID
step2.description: Show your Voter ID (EPIC) or any other approved alternate photo ID document to the polling officer.

step3.title: Get Inked
step3.description: The polling officer will mark your left index finger with indelible ink and take your signature/thumbprint.

step4.title: Press the Button
step4.description: Go to the voting compartment. Press the blue button on the EVM next to the symbol of your chosen candidate.

step5.title: Verify VVPAT
step5.description: Look at the VVPAT machine window. You will see a paper slip for 7 seconds verifying your vote was recorded.

step6.title: Vote Cast!
step6.description: Congratulations! You have successfully exercised your democratic right to vote.

RegisterPage.title: Voter Registration Guide
RegisterPage.subtitle: Check off the steps below to make sure you are ready to vote.

steps.step1: Check if your name is already on the voter list via the Voter Helpline App or NVSP.
steps.step2: If you are not registered, fill out Form 6 online or offline.
steps.step3: Keep your passport-size photo and address/age proof ready.
steps.step4: Submit the form and note down the reference ID for tracking.
steps.step5: Once approved, download your digital voter ID (e-EPIC).

QuizPage.title: Test Your Knowledge
QuizPage.subtitle: Take a simple quiz to see how much you know about your voting rights.

questions.q: At what age can an Indian citizen vote?
Which machine prints a paper slip to verify your vote?
Which form is used for registering as a new voter?

faqs.q: Can I vote if I lost my voter ID?
faqs.a: Yes! If your name is on the voter list, you can show an alternate photo ID like an Aadhaar card, PAN card, or Driving License.
No, voting is a democratic right and a civic duty, but it is not legally compulsory in India.

faqs.q: What happens if I press the wrong button on the EVM?
faqs.a: Once a button is pressed and the beep sounds, your vote is recorded and cannot be changed. Take your time to press the correct button.

faqs.q: Can I vote if I moved to a new city?
faqs.a: Yes, but you need to transfer your vote to your new constituency by filling out Form 8.
